---
title: "scratch 2"
output:
  pdf_document:
    latex_engine: xelatex
  html_document: default
  word_document: default
date: "2025-05-29"
---

# Introduction

```{r}
train <- read.csv("C:/Users/Jinxi Zhang/Desktop/project/training_data.csv", stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
test  <- read.csv("C:/Users/Jinxi Zhang/Desktop/project/test_data.csv",  stringsAsFactors=FALSE)

dim(train)  # 1120 × 11
dim(test)   #  280 × 10

sapply(train, function(x) sum(is.na(x)))

# Check ranges
summary(train$Age)                             

```

```{r}
str(train)
summary(train)
sapply(train, function(col) sum(is.na(col)))
```

# EDA

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# —— Credit.amount histograms and boxplots
p_hist_ca <- ggplot(train, aes(x = Credit.amount)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = ..density..),
                 bins   = 30,
                 fill   = "steelblue",
                 color  = "white",
                 alpha  = 0.6) +
  geom_density(color = "darkblue", size = 1) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Credit.amount distribution",
    x     = "Credit.amount",
    y     = "Density"
  )

# —— Duration histograms and boxplots
p_hist_dur <- ggplot(train, aes(x = Duration)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = ..density..),
                 bins  = 30,
                 fill  = "seagreen",
                 color = "white",
                 alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_density(color = "darkgreen", size = 1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Duration distribution",
    x     = "Duration (months)",
    y     = "Density"
  )

# —— Age histograms and boxplots
p_hist_age <- ggplot(train, aes(x = Age)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = ..density..),
                 bins  = 30,
                 fill  = "orchid",
                 color = "white",
                 alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_density(color = "darkmagenta", size = 1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Age distribution",
    x     = "Age",
    y     = "Density"
  )

# —— combined
final_histograms <- p_hist_ca | p_hist_dur | p_hist_age +
  plot_layout(ncol = 2, widths = c(1, 1, 1))

print(final_histograms)


```

```{r}
## categorical barplots
library(patchwork)
library(ggplot2)

# 1. Sex
p1 <- ggplot(train, aes(x = Sex)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "steelblue") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Sex", x = "", y = "Count") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# 2. Housing
p2 <- ggplot(train, aes(x = Housing)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "seagreen") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Housing", x = "", y = "Count") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# 3. Saving.accounts
p3 <- ggplot(train, aes(x = Saving.accounts)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "orchid") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Saving.accounts", x = "", y = "Count") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# 4. Checking.account
p4 <- ggplot(train, aes(x = Checking.account)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "lightcoral") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Checking.account", x = "", y = "Count") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# 5. Purpose
p5 <- ggplot(train, aes(x = Purpose)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "lightgoldenrod") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Purpose", x = "", y = "Count") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

(p1 + p2 + p3) / (p4 + p5) + plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1))
```
```{r}
## bivariate(risk)

library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# Numeric vs. Risk: Age, Duration, Credit.amount
for (v in c("Age", "Duration", "Credit.amount")) {
  p <- ggplot(train, aes_string(x = "Risk", y = v, fill = "Risk")) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = paste(v, "by Risk"), x = "Risk", y = v)
  
  # t-test to check if means differ significantly
  tt <- t.test(as.formula(paste(v, "~ Risk")), data = train)
  print(tt)
}

# 3. Categorical vs. Risk: Sex, Job, Housing, Saving.accounts, Checking.account, Purpose
for (v in c("Sex", "Job", "Housing", "Saving.accounts", "Checking.account", "Purpose")) {
  tbl <- table(train[[v]], train$Risk, useNA = "ifany")
  cat("\n\nVariable:", v, "\n")
  print(tbl)
  
  # Use χ² when all expected counts ≥ 5; otherwise Fisher’s exact
  chi <- chisq.test(tbl)
  if (all(chi$expected >= 5)) {
    print(chi)
  } else {
    print(fisher.test(tbl))
  }
}
  
# combined
p1 <- ggplot(train, aes(x = Risk, y = Age, fill = Risk)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Age by Risk", x = "Risk", y = "Age") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")  

p2 <- ggplot(train, aes(x = Risk, y = Duration, fill = Risk)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Duration by Risk", x = "Risk", y = "Duration") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

p3 <- ggplot(train, aes(x = Risk, y = Credit.amount, fill = Risk)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Credit.amount by Risk", x = "Risk", y = "Credit.amount") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

combined <- p1 + p2 + p3 + plot_layout(ncol = 3)
print(combined)

```

```{r}
## Targer variables (risk) ditribution
table(train$Risk)
prop.table(table(train$Risk))

ggplot(train, aes(x = Risk)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "steelblue") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Risk distribution", x = "Risk", y = "Count")

```

```{r}
true_labels <- read.csv("C:/Users/Jinxi Zhang/Desktop/project/kaggle_sample_solution_file.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
public_test <- test[test$ID %in% true_labels$ID, ]
public_test <- merge(public_test, true_labels, by = "ID", all.x = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# creaate LogCreditAmt、LogDuration
train$LogCreditAmt <- log1p(train$Credit.amount)
public_test$LogCreditAmt <- log1p(public_test$Credit.amount)

train$LogDuration <- log1p(train$Duration)
public_test$LogDuration <- log1p(public_test$Duration)

ggplot() +
  geom_histogram(data = train, aes(x = LogCreditAmt, y = ..density..),
                 bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_histogram(data = public_test, aes(x = LogCreditAmt, y = ..density..),
                 bins = 30, fill = "firebrick", alpha = 0.5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "LogCreditAmt 分布对比：训练集（蓝） vs 公共测试集（红）",
       x = "LogCreditAmt", y = "Density")

```

```{r}
# make NA to "unknown"
for (df in list(train, public_test)) {
  df$Purpose[df$Purpose == ""] <- NA
  df$Purpose[is.na(df$Purpose)] <- "unknown"
}

train$Purpose <- factor(train$Purpose)
public_test$Purpose <- factor(public_test$Purpose, levels = levels(train$Purpose))
```

```{r}

library(tidyverse)
blank_to_na <- function(x) {
  if (is.character(x)) {
    x <- ifelse(str_trim(x) == "" | str_to_lower(str_trim(x)) == "na", NA, x)
  }
  return(x)
}

# make character
train <- train %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.character), blank_to_na))

test <- test %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.character), blank_to_na))

```
```{r}
# Check remaining NA
sum(is.na(train$Saving.accounts))     
sum(is.na(train$Checking.account))  

train <- train %>%
  mutate(
    Saving.accounts   = replace_na(Saving.accounts, "unknown"),
    Checking.account  = replace_na(Checking.account,  "unknown")
  )

test <- test %>%
  mutate(
    Saving.accounts   = replace_na(Saving.accounts, "unknown"),
    Checking.account  = replace_na(Checking.account,  "unknown")
  )

```

```{r}
## Outliers procedure
## Credit.amount
# IQR
quantiles_amt <- quantile(train$Credit.amount, probs = c(0.25, 0.75), na.rm = TRUE)
Q1_amt <- quantiles_amt[1]
Q3_amt <- quantiles_amt[2]
IQR_amt <- Q3_amt - Q1_amt

upper_bound_amt <- Q3_amt + 3 * IQR_amt
lower_bound_amt <- Q1_amt - 3 * IQR_amt

upper_bound_amt
lower_bound_amt

train<- train %>%
  mutate(
    Credit.amount = ifelse(Credit.amount > upper_bound_amt, upper_bound_amt, Credit.amount),
    Credit.amount = ifelse(Credit.amount < lower_bound_amt, lower_bound_amt, Credit.amount)
  )

test <- test %>%
  mutate(
    Credit.amount = ifelse(Credit.amount > upper_bound_amt, upper_bound_amt, Credit.amount),
    Credit.amount = ifelse(Credit.amount < lower_bound_amt, lower_bound_amt, Credit.amount)
  )

quantiles_dur <- quantile(train$Duration, probs = c(0.25, 0.75), na.rm = TRUE)
Q1_dur <- quantiles_dur[1]
Q3_dur <- quantiles_dur[2]
IQR_dur <- Q3_dur - Q1_dur

upper_bound_dur <- Q3_dur + 3 * IQR_dur
lower_bound_dur <- Q1_dur - 3 * IQR_dur

train <- train %>%
  mutate(
    Duration = ifelse(Duration > upper_bound_dur, upper_bound_dur, Duration),
    Duration = ifelse(Duration < lower_bound_dur, lower_bound_dur, Duration)
  )

test <- test %>%
  mutate(
    Duration = ifelse(Duration > upper_bound_dur, upper_bound_dur, Duration),
    Duration = ifelse(Duration < lower_bound_dur, lower_bound_dur, Duration)
  )

upper_bound_dur
lower_bound_dur

## Age
age_quants <- quantile(train$Age, probs = c(0.25, 0.75), na.rm = TRUE)
Q1_age <- age_quants[1]
Q3_age <- age_quants[2]
IQR_age <- Q3_age - Q1_age

upper_bound_age <- Q3_age + 3 * IQR_age
lower_bound_age <- Q1_age - 3 * IQR_age

train <- train %>%
  mutate(
    Age = ifelse(Age > upper_bound_age, upper_bound_age, Age),
    Age = ifelse(Age < lower_bound_age, lower_bound_age, Age)
  )

test <- test %>%
  mutate(
    Age = ifelse(Age > upper_bound_age, upper_bound_age, Age),
    Age = ifelse(Age < lower_bound_age, lower_bound_age, Age)
  )

upper_bound_age
lower_bound_age
```

```{r}
## Combine rare categories
## Purpose
# check Purpose frequencies
purpose_counts <- train %>%
  count(Purpose) %>%
  arrange(n)
purpose_counts

# if frequencies less than 20, make them into "other"
rare_purposes <- purpose_counts %>% filter(n < 20) %>% pull(Purpose)

train <- train %>%
  mutate(
    Purpose = ifelse(Purpose %in% rare_purposes, "other", Purpose)
  )

test <- test %>%
  mutate(
    Purpose = ifelse(Purpose %in% rare_purposes, "other", Purpose)
  )

train$Purpose <- factor(train$Purpose)
purpose_levels <- levels(train$Purpose)

test$Purpose <- factor(test$Purpose, levels = purpose_levels)
test$Purpose <- fct_explicit_na(test$Purpose, na_level = "other")

```

```{r}
train$Sex <- factor(train$Sex)
test$Sex  <- factor(test$Sex, levels = levels(train$Sex))
test$Sex  <- fct_explicit_na(test$Sex, na_level = "unknown")

```

```{r}
# Create derived variables
train <- train %>%
  mutate(
    
    CreditPerMonth = Credit.amount / Duration,
    
    
    AgeGroup = cut(Age, 
                   breaks = c(0, 25, 35, 50, 100), 
                   labels = c("young", "middle", "senior", "old"),
                   include.lowest = TRUE),
    
    
    CreditGroup = cut(Credit.amount, 
                      breaks = quantile(Credit.amount, probs = c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1), na.rm = TRUE),
                      labels = c("low", "medium", "high", "very_high"),
                      include.lowest = TRUE),
    
    
    DurationGroup = cut(Duration,
                        breaks = c(0, 12, 24, 36, 100),
                        labels = c("short", "medium", "long", "very_long"),
                        include.lowest = TRUE),
    
   
    HighRiskProfile = as.numeric(
      Duration > 36 & 
      Checking.account %in% c("unknown", "little") &
      Saving.accounts %in% c("unknown", "little")
    ),
    
  
    AccountHealth = case_when(
      Checking.account == "rich" & Saving.accounts == "rich" ~ 4,
      Checking.account == "moderate" & Saving.accounts %in% c("moderate", "quite rich", "rich") ~ 3,
      Checking.account == "little" | Saving.accounts == "little" ~ 2,
      TRUE ~ 1
    )
  )


train_credit_breaks <- quantile(train$Credit.amount, probs = c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1), na.rm = TRUE)


test <- test %>%
  mutate(

    CreditPerMonth = Credit.amount / Duration,
    
   
    AgeGroup = cut(Age, 
                   breaks = c(0, 25, 35, 50, 100), 
                   labels = c("young", "middle", "senior", "old"),
                   include.lowest = TRUE),
    
    
    CreditGroup = cut(Credit.amount, 
                      breaks = train_credit_breaks,
                      labels = c("low", "medium", "high", "very_high"),
                      include.lowest = TRUE),
    
   
    DurationGroup = cut(Duration,
                        breaks = c(0, 12, 24, 36, 100),
                        labels = c("short", "medium", "long", "very_long"),
                        include.lowest = TRUE),
    
    
    HighRiskProfile = as.numeric(
      Duration > 36 & 
      Checking.account %in% c("unknown", "little") &
      Saving.accounts %in% c("unknown", "little")
    ),
    

    AccountHealth = case_when(
      Checking.account == "rich" & Saving.accounts == "rich" ~ 4,
      Checking.account == "moderate" & Saving.accounts %in% c("moderate", "quite rich", "rich") ~ 3,
      Checking.account == "little" | Saving.accounts == "little" ~ 2,
      TRUE ~ 1
    )
  )

```

# Normalization

```{r}

robust_scale <- function(x) {
  median_x <- median(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  iqr_x <- IQR(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  if (iqr_x == 0) {
   
    return((x - median_x) / sd(x, na.rm = TRUE))
  }
  return((x - median_x) / iqr_x)
}

train <- train %>%
  mutate(
   
    LogCreditAmt = log1p(Credit.amount),
    LogDuration = log1p(Duration),
    LogCreditPerMonth = log1p(CreditPerMonth)  
  )


robust_params <- train %>%
  summarise(
    median_log_amt = median(LogCreditAmt, na.rm = TRUE),
    iqr_log_amt = IQR(LogCreditAmt, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_log_dur = median(LogDuration, na.rm = TRUE),
    iqr_log_dur = IQR(LogDuration, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_age = median(Age, na.rm = TRUE),
    iqr_age = IQR(Age, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_log_cpm = median(LogCreditPerMonth, na.rm = TRUE),
    iqr_log_cpm = IQR(LogCreditPerMonth, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

train <- train %>%
  mutate(
    LogCreditAmt = (LogCreditAmt - robust_params$median_log_amt) / robust_params$iqr_log_amt,
    LogDuration = (LogDuration - robust_params$median_log_dur) / robust_params$iqr_log_dur,
    Age = (Age - robust_params$median_age) / robust_params$iqr_age,
    LogCreditPerMonth = (LogCreditPerMonth - robust_params$median_log_cpm) / robust_params$iqr_log_cpm
  )

test <- test %>%
  mutate(
    LogCreditAmt = log1p(Credit.amount),
    LogDuration = log1p(Duration),
    LogCreditPerMonth = log1p(CreditPerMonth)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    LogCreditAmt = (LogCreditAmt - robust_params$median_log_amt) / robust_params$iqr_log_amt,
    LogDuration = (LogDuration - robust_params$median_log_dur) / robust_params$iqr_log_dur,
    Age = (Age - robust_params$median_age) / robust_params$iqr_age,
    LogCreditPerMonth = (LogCreditPerMonth - robust_params$median_log_cpm) / robust_params$iqr_log_cpm
  )
```

# new data name

```{r}
train_cleaned <- train %>%
  select(
    ID,
    
    LogCreditAmt, LogDuration, Age, LogCreditPerMonth,  
    Sex, Job, Housing, Saving.accounts, Checking.account, Purpose,
    AgeGroup, CreditGroup, DurationGroup,
    HighRiskProfile, AccountHealth,
    Risk
  )

test_cleaned <- test %>%
  select(
    ID,
    LogCreditAmt, LogDuration, Age, LogCreditPerMonth,  # 移除了 CreditPerMonth_scaled
    Sex, Job, Housing, Saving.accounts, Checking.account, Purpose,
    AgeGroup, CreditGroup, DurationGroup,
    HighRiskProfile, AccountHealth
  )
```

# Make sure dummy columns align

```{r}
library(fastDummies)

train_dummy <- fastDummies::dummy_cols(
  train_cleaned,
  select_columns = c("Sex", "Job", "Housing", "Saving.accounts", 
                     "Checking.account", "Purpose", "AgeGroup", 
                     "CreditGroup", "DurationGroup"),  
  remove_first_dummy = TRUE,
  remove_selected_columns = TRUE
)

if (!("ID" %in% names(train_dummy))) {
  stop("train_dummy 中没有 ID")
}

train_dummy_cols <- names(train_dummy)
model_features <- setdiff(train_dummy_cols, c("ID", "Risk"))

test_dummy <- fastDummies::dummy_cols(
  test_cleaned,
  select_columns = c("Sex", "Job", "Housing", "Saving.accounts", 
                     "Checking.account", "Purpose", "AgeGroup", 
                     "CreditGroup", "DurationGroup"),
  remove_first_dummy = TRUE,
  remove_selected_columns = TRUE
)

test_dummy_cols <- names(test_dummy)
missing_cols <- setdiff(model_features, test_dummy_cols)
for (colname in missing_cols) {
  test_dummy[[colname]] <- 0L
}

extra_cols <- setdiff(test_dummy_cols, c("ID", model_features))
if (length(extra_cols) > 0) {
  test_dummy <- test_dummy %>% select(-all_of(extra_cols))
}

test_dummy <- test_dummy %>%
  select(ID, all_of(model_features))

head(test_dummy)

```

```{r}
final_train_feats <- names(train_dummy)[names(train_dummy) != "Risk"]
final_test_feats  <- names(test_dummy)
identical(final_train_feats, final_test_feats)
```

# Make sure normalization is done correctly

```{r}
summary(select(train_dummy, LogCreditAmt, LogDuration, Age, LogCreditPerMonth))
summary(select(test_dummy,  LogCreditAmt, LogDuration, Age, LogCreditPerMonth))
```

# Characteristics 

```{r}
prop.table(table(train_dummy$HighRiskProfile, train_dummy$Risk), margin = 1)
prop.table(table(train_dummy$AccountHealth, train_dummy$Risk), margin = 1)


cat("训练集列数:", ncol(train_dummy), "\n")
cat("测试集列数:", ncol(test_dummy), "\n")
cat("特征是否对齐:", identical(names(train_dummy)[-ncol(train_dummy)], names(test_dummy)), "\n")

cat("\n=== 新特征分布 ===\n")
cat("HighRiskProfile 分布:\n")
table(train_dummy$HighRiskProfile)

cat("\nAccountHealth 分布:\n")
table(train_dummy$AccountHealth)

cat("\n=== 缺失值检查 ===\n")
cat("训练集缺失值:", sum(is.na(train_dummy)), "\n")
cat("测试集缺失值:", sum(is.na(test_dummy)), "\n")
```

# Modeling

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(caret)
library(glmnet)
library(pROC)
library(ranger)
library(xgboost)

train_dummy$Risk <- factor(train_dummy$Risk, levels = c("good","bad"))

X <- train_dummy %>% select(-ID, -Risk)
y <- train_dummy$Risk



set.seed(123)  
train_idx <- createDataPartition(y, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
X_train    <- X[train_idx, ]
y_train    <- y[train_idx]
X_valid    <- X[-train_idx, ]
y_valid    <- y[-train_idx]

prop.table(table(y_train))
prop.table(table(y_valid))

cv_ctrl <- trainControl(
  method = "repeatedcv", 
  number = 5,
  repeats = 3,
  summaryFunction = twoClassSummary,
  classProbs = TRUE,
  savePredictions = "final",
  verboseIter = FALSE
)
```

# Model 1：ElasticNet Logistic Regression ——

```{R}
tmp <- train(
  x = X_train, y = y_train,
  method = "glmnet",
  family = "binomial",
  trControl = trainControl(method = "none"),
  tuneGrid = expand.grid(alpha = 0, lambda = 0)
)
lambda_seq <- tmp$finalModel$lambda

glmnet_grid <- expand.grid(
  alpha = c(0, 0.5, 1),
  lambda = lambda_seq[c(10, 50, 100, length(lambda_seq))]
)

set.seed(123)
lr_model_v2 <- train(
  x = X_train, 
  y = y_train,
  method = "glmnet",
  family = "binomial",
  metric = "ROC",
  tuneGrid = glmnet_grid,
  trControl = cv_ctrl
)
print(lr_model_v2$bestTune)
cat("ElasticNet CV AUC:", max(lr_model_v2$results$ROC), "\n")
prob_lr <- predict(lr_model_v2, X_valid, type = "prob")[, "bad"]

pred_lr <- factor(ifelse(prob_lr >= 0.5, "bad", "good"), levels = c("good", "bad"))

cm_lr <- confusionMatrix(pred_lr, y_valid, positive = "bad")
print(cm_lr)
```

# Model 2：Random Forest ——

```{r}
rf_grid <- expand.grid(
  mtry = c(3, 5, 7), 
  splitrule = "gini",
  min.node.size = c(20, 30, 50)  
)

set.seed(123)
rf_model_v2 <- train(
  x = X_train, 
  y = y_train,
  method = "ranger",
  metric = "ROC",
  tuneGrid = rf_grid, 
  trControl = cv_ctrl,
  num.trees = 500,
  importance = "impurity"
)

print(rf_model_v2$bestTune)
cat("Random Forest CV AUC:", max(rf_model_v2$results$ROC), "\n")
```

# Model 3：XGBoost ——

```{r}
xgb_grid <- expand.grid(
  nrounds = c(50, 100),  
  max_depth = c(3, 4),   
  eta = c(0.05, 0.1),    
  gamma = c(0, 1, 2),    
  colsample_bytree = 0.7,
  min_child_weight = c(5, 10), 
  subsample = 0.7
)

set.seed(123)
xgb_model_v2 <- train(
  x = X_train, 
  y = y_train,
  method = "xgbTree",
  metric = "ROC",
  tuneGrid = xgb_grid,  
  trControl = cv_ctrl,
  verbose = FALSE
)

print(xgb_model_v2$bestTune)
cat("XGBoost CV AUC:", max(xgb_model_v2$results$ROC), "\n")
prob_xgb <- predict(xgb_model_v2, X_valid, type = "prob")[, "bad"]


pred_xgb <- factor(ifelse(prob_xgb >= 0.5, "bad", "good"), levels = c("good", "bad"))


cm_xgb <- confusionMatrix(pred_xgb, y_valid, positive = "bad")
print(cm_xgb)
```

# Combined

```{r}
best_rf <- rf_model_v2$bestTune
train_rf_df <- data.frame(X_train, Risk = y_train)

set.seed(123)
rf_fit_v2 <- ranger(
  formula = Risk ~ .,
  data = train_rf_df,
  num.trees = 500,
  mtry = best_rf$mtry,
  splitrule = best_rf$splitrule,
  min.node.size = best_rf$min.node.size,
  probability = TRUE,
  importance = "impurity"
)

valid_rf_df <- data.frame(X_valid)  
valid_rf_probs <- predict(rf_fit_v2, data = valid_rf_df)$predictions[, "bad"]

y_valid_num <- as.numeric(y_valid) - 1
auc_rf_v2 <- roc(response = y_valid_num, predictor = valid_rf_probs)$auc
cat("Random Forest 验证集 AUC (v2):", round(auc_rf_v2, 4), "\n")


valid_pred_rf <- ifelse(valid_rf_probs > 0.5, "bad", "good")
valid_pred_rf <- factor(valid_pred_rf, levels = c("good","bad"))
cm_rf_v2 <- confusionMatrix(valid_pred_rf, y_valid, positive = "bad")
print(cm_rf_v2)


best_xgb <- xgb_model_v2$bestTune
x_train_mat <- as.matrix(X_train)
x_valid_mat <- as.matrix(X_valid)
y_train_num <- as.numeric(y_train) - 1

dtrain <- xgb.DMatrix(data = x_train_mat, label = y_train_num)
dvalid <- xgb.DMatrix(data = x_valid_mat, label = y_valid_num)

xgb_params <- list(
  objective = "binary:logistic",
  eval_metric = "auc",
  max_depth = best_xgb$max_depth,
  eta = best_xgb$eta,
  gamma = best_xgb$gamma,
  colsample_bytree = best_xgb$colsample_bytree,
  min_child_weight = best_xgb$min_child_weight,
  subsample = best_xgb$subsample
)

set.seed(123)
xgb_fit_v2 <- xgb.train(
  params = xgb_params,
  data = dtrain,
  nrounds = best_xgb$nrounds,
  watchlist = list(train = dtrain, valid = dvalid),
  early_stopping_rounds = 20,
  verbose = 0
)

valid_xgb_probs <- predict(xgb_fit_v2, x_valid_mat)
auc_xgb_v2 <- roc(response = y_valid_num, predictor = valid_xgb_probs)$auc
cat("XGBoost 验证集 AUC (v2):", round(auc_xgb_v2, 4), "\n")


best_lr <- lr_model_v2$bestTune
set.seed(123)
enet_fit_v2 <- glmnet(
  x = x_train_mat,
  y = y_train_num,
  family = "binomial",
  alpha = best_lr$alpha,
  lambda = best_lr$lambda
)

valid_lr_probs <- predict(enet_fit_v2, newx = x_valid_mat, type = "response")[,1]
auc_lr_v2 <- roc(response = y_valid_num, predictor = valid_lr_probs)$auc
cat("ElasticNet 验证集 AUC (v2):", round(auc_lr_v2, 4), "\n")


cat("\n=== AUC对比总结 ===\n")
cat("模型              原版本    新版本\n")
cat("Random Forest:    0.9431   ", round(auc_rf_v2, 4), "\n")
cat("XGBoost:          0.9190   ", round(auc_xgb_v2, 4), "\n")
cat("ElasticNet:       0.7611   ", round(auc_lr_v2, 4), "\n")
```


```{r}
X_all <- train_dummy %>% select(-ID, -Risk)
y_all <- train_dummy$Risk

train_all_df <- data.frame(X_all, Risk = y_all)

set.seed(123)
rf_final_v2 <- ranger(
  formula = Risk ~ .,
  data = train_all_df,
  num.trees = 500,
  mtry = best_rf$mtry,
  splitrule = best_rf$splitrule,
  min.node.size = best_rf$min.node.size,
  probability = TRUE,
  importance = "impurity"
)

x_all_mat <- as.matrix(X_all)
y_all_num <- as.numeric(y_all) - 1
dall <- xgb.DMatrix(data = x_all_mat, label = y_all_num)

set.seed(123)
xgb_final_v2 <- xgb.train(
  params = xgb_params,
  data = dall,
  nrounds = best_xgb$nrounds,
  verbose = 0
)


public_labels <- read.csv(
  "C:/Users/Jinxi Zhang/Desktop/project/kaggle_sample_solution_file.csv",
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

#
public_test <- test_dummy %>%
  inner_join(public_labels, by = "ID") %>%
  mutate(Risk = factor(Risk, levels = c("good", "bad")))

if("Usage" %in% names(public_test)) {
  public_test <- public_test %>% select(-Usage)
}

X_public <- public_test %>% select(-ID, -Risk)
y_public <- public_test$Risk


public_rf_df <- data.frame(X_public)
public_rf_probs_v2 <- predict(rf_final_v2, data = public_rf_df)$predictions[, "bad"]


y_public_num <- as.numeric(y_public) - 1
auc_public_rf_v2 <- roc(response = y_public_num, predictor = public_rf_probs_v2)$auc
cat("\n=== Random Forest 公共测试集AUC ===\n")
cat("原版本: 0.4648\n")
cat("新版本:", round(auc_public_rf_v2, 4), "\n")


x_public_mat <- as.matrix(X_public)
public_xgb_probs_v2 <- predict(xgb_final_v2, x_public_mat)
auc_public_xgb_v2 <- roc(response = y_public_num, predictor = public_xgb_probs_v2)$auc
cat("\n=== XGBoost 公共测试集AUC ===\n")
cat("新版本:", round(auc_public_xgb_v2, 4), "\n")


ensemble_probs <- 0.5 * public_rf_probs_v2 + 0.5 * public_xgb_probs_v2
auc_ensemble <- roc(response = y_public_num, predictor = ensemble_probs)$auc
cat("\n=== 集成模型 公共测试集AUC ===\n")
cat("RF+XGB集成:", round(auc_ensemble, 4), "\n")
```
```{r}
# 10. 查看并可视化特征重要性
#     importance() 会返回每个特征的 Gini 增益总和
rf_imp <- importance(rf_final_v2)

# 11. 将特征重要性整理为 data.frame，并按降序排列
imp_df <- data.frame(
  Feature    = names(rf_imp),
  Importance = as.numeric(rf_imp),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>%
  arrange(desc(Importance))

# 12. 打印最重要的前 10 个特征
print(head(imp_df, 10))

# 13. 绘制 Top 10 特征的重要性条形图
ggplot(head(imp_df, 10), aes(x = reorder(Feature, Importance), y = Importance)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Random Forest 特征重要性 Top 10 (V2.3)",
    x     = "特征",
    y     = "重要性 (Gini 增益)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
